topNews.title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Artificial intelligence may be dominating boardroom agendas, but many enterprises are discovering that the biggest obstacle to meaningful adoption is the state of their data. While consumer-facing AI tools have dazzled users with speed and ease, enterprise leaders are discovering that deploying AI a…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Enterprises face challenges in adopting AI due to inadequate data infrastructure.  
• Why it matters: Effective AI deployment hinges on high-quality, well-structured data for meaningful insights.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can invest in modernizing data stacks to enhance AI integration and drive business value.

topNews.title: LLMs are not a higher level of abstraction
topNews.rawTextSnippet: LLMs are not a higher level of abstraction
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A discussion emerged asserting that large language models (LLMs) do not represent a higher level of abstraction in AI.  
• Why it matters: This challenges the perception of LLMs as advanced AI, impacting their adoption and integration in various sectors.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can explore alternative AI models or enhancements to LLMs that truly elevate abstraction and functionality.

topNews.title: Show HN: OSS Agent I built topped the TerminalBench on Gemini-3-flash-preview
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Scored 65.2% vs google's official 47.8%, and the existing top closed source model Junie CLI's 64.3%. Since there are a lot of reports of deliberate cheating on TerminalBench 2.0 lately ( https://debugml.github.io/cheating-agents/ ), I would like to also clarify a few th…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: An open-source agent outperformed Google's model and a leading closed-source competitor in TerminalBench testing.  
• Why it matters: This achievement highlights the potential of open-source solutions in competitive AI benchmarks, challenging proprietary models.  
• Business opportunity: There is a growing market for developing and promoting open-source AI tools that can rival established, closed-source products.

funding.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
funding.rawTextSnippet: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

dailyBrief: Today's AI landscape is marked by significant shifts, notably with SWE-bench Verified no longer assessing frontier coding capabilities, raising questions about benchmarking standards in software development. Meanwhile, a tiny Polish startup has emerged as a multi-billion-dollar player in the AI voice sector, highlighting the potential for rapid growth in niche markets. In a strategic move, OpenAI has restructured its partnership with Microsoft, instituting a cap on revenue sharing, which could impact future collaborations and investment strategies. Additionally, the MIT Technology Review emphasizes the need for businesses to rebuild their data stacks to effectively harness AI, underscoring the importance of robust data infrastructure in driving innovation and competitive advantage.
